assertions.title: Floor threshold as metadata not hard filter

steps.action: Run FVGDetector on 5m with floor_threshold_pips=0.5, verify sub-floor FVGs are emitted and only metadata tracks the split
steps.expected: 345 total FVGs including sub-floor ones; floor is metadata annotation only
steps.observed: 345 total: 109 sub-floor (gap_pips < 0.5) + 236 above-floor; metadata correctly tracks above_floor_count=236, below_floor_count=109

evidence.pythonOutput: VAL-FVG-007 (5m): total=345, sub_floor=109, above_floor=236. Floor threshold is metadata/stats only, not a hard filter. PASS

frictions.description: The task prompt's setup code uses 'aggregate_tf' but the actual function name is 'aggregate'. Also, config params are Pydantic models (not dicts), so params must be passed as a flat dict {'floor_threshold_pips': 0.5} rather than using config.primitives['fvg'].params directly.
frictions.resolution: Used correct import 'from ra.data.tf_aggregator import aggregate' and flat dict params as shown in existing test_fvg.py

summary: Tested 7 assertions: all 7 passed. FVG detector produces exact regression counts on 1m (2017), 5m (345), 15m (118). Per-detection fields match baseline within 1e-6 tolerance. IFVG state transitions (CE_TOUCHED, BOUNDARY_CLOSED) match baseline across all TFs. BPR overlap zones have correct boundaries. Floor threshold is metadata-only annotation — 109 sub-floor FVGs emitted on 5m.
